OneDrive processes running — cloud sync root active. CfAPI filter driver (CldFlt.sys) is loaded.

commands.command:
# Cloud files architecture:
# CldFlt.sys = filter driver (kernel)
# cldapi.dll = user-mode API
# OneDrive.exe = sync engine (user)
# Sync root = registered directory tree
commands.output:
Layer 0: NTFS (underlying filesystem)
Layer 1: CldFlt.sys (filter driver)
Layer 2: cldapi.dll (user API)
Layer 3: OneDrive.exe (sync engine)

Reparse points mark cloud-stored files.
Local files: no reparse tag.
Cloud files: reparse tag + placeholder.
commands.note: Understanding the stack is critical — fuzzing the wrong layer gives false positives.

commands.command:
# CfAPI fuzzing targets:
# CfCreatePlaceholders — needs registered sync root
# CfUpdatePlaceholder — updates file metadata
# CfExecute — transfers data between local and cloud
# CfHydratePlaceholder — downloads cloud file to local
# CfDehydratePlaceholder — removes local copy, keeps cloud
commands.output:
API Fuzzing Results:

CfCreatePlaceholders:
  Without sync root: 0x80070157 (ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER)
  With sync root: SUCCESS (placeholder created)

CfUpdatePlaceholder:
  Random paths: 0x80070057 (ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER)
  Valid placeholder: SUCCESS

CfExecute:
  Without proper handle: 0x80070057
  With valid handle: SUCCESS

CfHydratePlaceholder:
  Without placeholder: 0x80070006 (ERROR_INVALID_HANDLE)
  With placeholder: SUCCESS (file downloaded)
commands.note: All APIs require proper sync root registration. Random temp directories fail.

commands.command:
# TOCTOU race condition theory:
# Time-of-check: Verify file state (placeholder, hydrated, etc.)
# Time-of-use: Perform operation on that file
# Race window: Between check and use, file state changes
commands.output:
Race Condition Scenario:

Thread A: Check if file is hydrated (yes)
Thread B: Dehydrate the file (now it's not)
Thread A: Read file content (FAIL — file is gone)

In cloud files:
Thread A: Check placeholder state
Thread B: Update placeholder via CfUpdatePlaceholder
Thread A: Execute transfer via CfExecute

Result: Operation on stale state = undefined behavior
commands.note: TOCTOU in kernel drivers is especially dangerous — can lead to memory corruption or privilege escalation.

commands.note: Driver Verifier catches silent kernel corruption that normal execution hides.

commands.command:
# What Driver Verifier catches:
# - Memory corruption in driver buffers
# - IRQL violations (raising when should lower)
# - Pool leaks (allocate without free)
# - Deadlocks (circular lock acquisition)
# - DDI compliance (wrong API usage)
commands.output:
Without Verifier:
  Kernel bug → silent corruption → system unstable
  May crash hours later, unrelated stack trace

With Verifier:
  Kernel bug → immediate BSOD with DRIVER_VERIFIER_DETECTED_VIOLATION
  Stack trace points to actual offender
  Reproducible, debuggable, fixable
commands.note: BSOD with Verifier is GOOD — it means you found the bug before it found you.

commands.output:
1. Enumerate sync roots on your machine:
   Get-ChildItem -Path $env:OneDrive -Recurse | Where-Object { $_.Attributes -match 'ReparsePoint' }

2. Inspect a reparse point:
   fsutil reparsepoint query <file_path>

3. Register a test sync root (requires admin):
   CfRegisterSyncRoot with proper CF_SYNC_ROOT_REGISTRATION

4. Create a placeholder and observe:
   CfCreatePlaceholders with CF_PLACEHOLDER_CREATE_INFO

5. Trigger a race condition:
   Thread A: Check placeholder state
   Thread B: Update placeholder
   Thread A: Execute transfer

6. Enable Driver Verifier on CldFlt.sys:
   verifier /standard /driver CldFlt.sys
   Reboot and test

7. Analyze any crashes with WinDbg:
   !analyze -v
   !verifier 3

8. BONUS: Find a reproducible crash and document the primitive
commands.note: .42 has Driver Verifier active — any kernel bug will BSOD immediately. Document the crash and analyze with WinDbg.
